content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and SSS.

Find any array A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] of length NNN such that:


Each AiA_iAi​ is a **positive** integer.
The sum of the array is SSS, i.e, A1+A2+…+AN=SA_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_N = SA1​+A2​+…+AN​=S.
The bitwise XOR of the array is 000, i.e, A1⊕A2⊕…⊕AN=0A_1\oplus A_2\oplus\ldots\oplus A_N = 0A1​⊕A2​⊕…⊕AN​=0.
If multiple valid arrays exist, you can find any of them.

If no valid array exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS — the length of the array and the desired sum.


Output Format
For each test case:



If no valid array exists, output a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise output NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array you found.

If multiple possible valid arrays exist, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the array A=[7,1,10,12]A = [7, 1, 10, 12]A=[7,1,10,12]. The sum of this array is 7+1+10+12=307+1+10+12 = 307+1+10+12=30, and its bitwise XOR is 7⊕1⊕10⊕12=07\oplus 1\oplus 10\oplus 12 = 07⊕1⊕10⊕12=0, as required.
Test case 222: Consider the array A=[5,3,6]A = [5, 3, 6]A=[5,3,6]. The sum of this array is 5+3+6=145+3+6=145+3+6=14, and its bitwise XOR is 5⊕3⊕6=05\oplus 3\oplus 6 = 05⊕3⊕6=0, as required.
Test case 333: No valid array of length 222, sum 555, and XOR 000 exists.
